Assembly: The assembly ilArtEvon1.hap1.1 is based on 103x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 27 (3.8 - 4.6 Mbp) and chromosome 28 (3.1 - 3.5 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly icCteCupr1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Sex chromosomes were determined by read-coverage statistics and HiC data.

Assembly: The assembly ilHetNeph1.hap1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly.

Assembly: The assembly daArnMont1.hap1.1 is based on 65x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Heterozygous structural variation was observed in the following regions: chromosome 1, 34.25 Mb to 62.8 Mb; chromosome 4, 25.6 Mb to 29.7 Mb; chromosome 6, 28.8 Mb to 46.3 Mb; chromosome 18; 10.4 Mb to 12.4 Mb and chromosome 19, 14.3 Mb to 21.9 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly mSpeCit3.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas pilot project~(https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/pilot-project). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. The order and orientation of contigs in Chromosome 12 between approximately 23.6-68.8Mb is unsure.Chromosome X identified by alignment to GCA_902686455.2 and GCA_902686445.2.

Assembly: The assembly ilMacLitu1.2 is based on 54x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. SUPER_5 region 8.34 - 9.38 Mbp is highly repetitive and order and orientation may be uncertain. Chromosome Z was assigned by synteny to Macaria notata GCA_927399415.1.
